methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $300M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $12.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Thinking Machines Lab
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $12B

private.name: Sakana AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Periodic Labs is a Seed-stage AI & Software company founded by Liam Fedus and Ekin Dogus Cubuk that has raised $300M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer set of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies spanning both Seed and Series A rounds, with one public comparable (Palantir, PLTR) and eight private peers including Thinking Machines Lab, Sakana AI, and others in the frontier AI space.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method uses the $300M raised as a value proxy and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage progression, yielding $750M. The Peer-Median method examines five comparable-stage companies with valuations ranging from $250M (Twelve Labs) to $12.0B (Thinking Machines Lab), producing a median valuation of $2.0B. The dispersion across peers reflects variance in capital efficiency and investor appetite within early-stage frontier AI.

Triangulating these two approaches yields a valuation range of $638M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $1.4B. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around whether Periodic Labs tracks closer to lower-funded Series A comparables like Twelve Labs or better-capitalized Seed peers like Thinking Machines Lab. Stage progression and peer median methods diverge by approximately 167%, suggesting that peer positioning and future capital deployment assumptions materially drive valuation outcomes.
